# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

broker_id: <your-kafka-broker-ID> # Should be unique for each broker within kafka cluster, not static and can't exceed 1024. Must match between brokers in your zookeeper ensemble (use the id of this machine as KAFKA_ADVERTISED_PORT if you wish to use it only once)
log_dirs: /path/to/your-kafka-data # Path where kafka data is stored. It'll be periodically purged by log retention policy, but can hold recent logs indefinitely (default : -1 for disabling). 0 indicates not to use or enable
listeners: "PLAINTEXT://your_kafka_brokerIPAddress:port" # The kafka listener will accept messages from clients at this endpoint. Should be a string of host/port pairs, comma separated (default : 'plaintext://localhost:9092') 
advertised_listeners: "PLAINTEXT://your-kafka-brokerIPAddress:port" # If you want your client to connect using different broker address set it here. It should be the same as advertised in listeners, must match for clients connected (default : 'plaintext://localhost:9092')
zookeeper_connect: "yourZookeeprIPAddress1:port,another-ipaddress/hostname1=port" # This is a string of host:/port pairs to form the initial set of known hosts. Use this if you want clients (i.e., producers or consumers) notified when Kafka becomes available by setting enable_auto_recover = 'true' in your client properties, must be same as zookeeper connect
unclean_leader_election: "false" # Enable leader election using the configuration property if you want to make sure that only one broker can maintain its leadership. When enabled it means multiple requests for control of partition leaders will fail (default : false) 
log4j_statusdir: /path/to/your-kafka/data -1, enable or disable logging using log4J with status and metrics reports at regular intervals to the given path location from where Kafka stores it. If you want no logs for this use '-1' as directory
log4j_dirs: /path/to/your-kafka/data # Path in which your custom logger (org.apache.logging.log4j) will write the log data, again with a status report to given path location from where Kafka stores it if enable is set or no logs for this use '-1' as directory
